name: Django CSRF Protection Disabled on API Views
description: Django API view decorated with @csrf_exempt or using CsrfExemptSessionAuthentication without additional request origin validation allows cross-site request forgery attacks where attacker-controlled pages submit state-changing requests on behalf of authenticated users.
mitigations: Use token-based authentication (JWT, DRF TokenAuth) with Authorization header instead of cookies for APIs
If session auth is required, keep CSRF enabled and validate Origin header
Set SameSite=Strict or Lax on session cookies to limit cross-site delivery
Audit all @csrf_exempt decorators and require justification in code review

name: FastAPI Missing Auth Dependency on Protected Routes
description: Developer omitting the FastAPI Depends(get_current_user) security dependency from a route that was intended to be protected allows unauthenticated access to administrative or user-specific endpoints.
mitigations: Apply authentication dependencies at the router level using APIRouter(dependencies=[Depends(auth)]) to prevent per-route omissions
Use OpenAPI security scheme annotations so auth requirements are visible and auditable
Write integration tests asserting 401 on all protected routes without credentials
Add a CI check that lists all routes and verifies each has an auth dependency

name: Express.js Prototype Pollution via Request Body
description: Express application using body-parser with JSON payloads allows attackers to send objects containing __proto__ or constructor keys, polluting the JavaScript object prototype and overriding application-wide security properties or function behavior.
mitigations: Use JSON schema validation (ajv with allowUnionTypes:false) to reject payloads with __proto__ keys
Configure body-parser with a custom reviver that blocks prototype pollution keys
Keep Node.js updated — recent versions include prototype pollution mitigations
Use a linter rule to flag Object.assign or lodash.merge with untrusted objects

name: Rails Mass Assignment Without Strong Parameters
description: Rails controller passing params.permit! or using hash assignment without strong parameters allows attackers to overwrite any model attribute including privileged fields (admin, role, balance), even if not present in the HTML form.
mitigations: Use params.require(:model).permit(:field1, :field2) to explicitly allowlist writable attributes
Never use params.permit! in production code
Configure attr_accessible in models as a second line of defense
Write controller tests with unexpected parameter injection to verify rejection

name: Next.js Server Action Exposed Without Authorization
description: Next.js Server Action that modifies data or triggers privileged operations without checking the caller's session allows any authenticated (or unauthenticated) user to invoke the action directly via HTTP POST to the action endpoint.
mitigations: Validate session and user role at the top of every Server Action before performing any operation
Use middleware to enforce authentication for all Server Action routes
Treat Server Actions as untrusted API endpoints — apply the same security controls as REST routes
Audit Server Action exports with automated tooling to ensure auth checks are present

name: Django Debug Mode Enabled in Production
description: Django application deployed with DEBUG=True exposes full stack traces, local variables, SQL queries, and installed app configurations in error pages, providing attackers with detailed intelligence about the application internals.
mitigations: Set DEBUG=False in all production and staging environments
Use environment variables or secrets management for Django settings — never hardcode DEBUG=True
Configure ALLOWED_HOSTS to prevent Django from serving responses to unexpected domains
Add a startup check that exits if DEBUG=True is detected outside of local development

name: FastAPI Response Model Leaking Internal Fields
description: FastAPI endpoint returning a SQLAlchemy model or Pydantic model without an explicit response_model exposes all fields including internal ones (hashed_password, secret_key, internal_flag), since FastAPI serializes the full object by default.
mitigations: Always specify response_model on FastAPI route decorators to enforce output shape
Create dedicated Pydantic response schemas that explicitly exclude sensitive fields
Never return ORM model objects directly — always convert to response schemas
Add integration tests that assert sensitive fields are absent from API responses

name: Express.js Path Traversal in Static File Serving
description: Express.js application serving user-supplied file paths without normalization allows attackers to use path traversal sequences (../../etc/passwd) to read files outside the intended static directory, including application secrets and configuration files.
mitigations: Use path.resolve and validate the resolved path starts with the expected base directory
Prefer express.static() for static files — it handles traversal prevention internally
Never concatenate user input directly into file system paths
Run file-serving logic under a least-privilege OS user with restricted directory access

name: Rails ActiveRecord Raw SQL Injection
description: Rails controller using Model.where("field = '#{params[:value]}'") with string interpolation instead of parameterized conditions allows SQL injection, enabling attackers to read arbitrary data, bypass authentication, or manipulate records.
mitigations: Use parameterized conditions: Model.where('field = ?', params[:value]) or Model.where(field: params[:value])
Enable Brakeman static analysis in CI to flag unsafe ActiveRecord patterns
Avoid raw SQL fragments in scopes, find_by_sql, and joins without parameterization
Use Rails' sanitize_sql_* helpers when raw SQL is unavoidable

name: Next.js Middleware Auth Bypass via Malformed URL
description: Next.js middleware applying authentication checks based on pathname matching can be bypassed by supplying URLs with encoded characters, trailing slashes, or case variations that do not match the expected pattern but still route to the protected page.
mitigations: Use Next.js matcher config with explicit path patterns rather than manual pathname comparison
Normalize the request URL (decode, lowercase, strip trailing slashes) before matching
Verify auth on the page/API route level as a defense-in-depth measure, not only in middleware
Write end-to-end tests with URL variations to validate middleware coverage

name: Django Admin Interface Exposed Without IP Restriction
description: Django admin interface accessible from the public internet without IP allowlisting exposes the full administrative UI to brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, and zero-day exploits targeting Django admin functionality.
mitigations: Restrict /admin/ via firewall rules or middleware to internal IP ranges or VPN
Use a non-default URL prefix for the admin interface to reduce automated scanning
Enforce MFA for all Django admin accounts
Monitor admin login attempts and alert on failed login spikes

name: FastAPI Request Body Size Limit Not Enforced
description: FastAPI application without request body size limits allows attackers to send arbitrarily large JSON payloads that exhaust server memory during parsing, causing out-of-memory errors or application crashes under moderate load.
mitigations: Configure uvicorn or the ASGI server with a maximum request body size limit
Use a reverse proxy (nginx, Caddy) to enforce size limits before requests reach FastAPI
Validate Content-Length header before reading the body in sensitive endpoints
Set timeouts on body reads to prevent slow-upload attacks
